forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,63 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- wballard
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: f680
+title: Lenient tool-call JSON parsing in llama-agent (strip fences, drop think tags, accept multiple wrappers)
+---
+**Observed:** Qwen3.6-27B (via `unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF`) emitted a tool-call attempt that the validator runner saw as raw text:
+
+```
+<think>
+The user wants me to validate ... I need to read the file ...
+</think>
+
+```json
+{"call_tool":{"arguments":"{\"file_path\": \"...sample_avp_test.rs\"}","name":"read_file"}}
+```
+```
+
+llama-agent's `Default` strategy (chosen because the model name `Qwen3.6-27B` does not match the `qwen3 + coder` predicate in `detect_from_model_name`) ran JsonToolCallParser, XmlToolCallParser, FunctionCallParser in sequence and matched none of them â€” so the agent reported "No tool calls detected" and the text bubbled all the way up to `avp_common::validator::executor` as the final assistant response.
+
+**Where it lives:** `llama-agent/src/chat_template.rs` â€” `ToolParsingStrategy` enum, `detect_from_model_name`, the parsers registered in `ChatTemplateEngine::new()` (`json`, `xml`, `function_call`).
+
+**Two compounding parser-coverage gaps:**
+
+1. **JSON parser is brittle about wrapping.** Models in practice emit JSON inside markdown code fences (```json â€¦ ```), inside `<think>â€¦</think>` reasoning blocks (Qwen3 series), interleaved with prose, or with leading/trailing whitespace and natural-language explanation. A "JSON parser" worth the name should be forgiving in what it accepts. Today, anything other than a bare top-level JSON object is missed.
+
+2. **Wrapper-shape coverage is incomplete.** Even after stripping fences/think tags, the JSON object inside can take several shapes in the wild:
+   - **Hermes / canonical Qwen3:** `{"name": "...", "arguments": {...}}` â€” the format Qwen3.6 emits when the chat template injects tool schemas. Wrapped in `<tool_call>...</tool_call>` per the tokenizer config. ([qwen.readthedocs.io function_call docs](https://qwen.readthedocs.io/en/latest/framework/function_call.html), [vLLM tool_call_parser hermes](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/features/tool_calling/))
+   - **OpenAI legacy function_call:** `{"function_call": {"name": "...", "arguments": "<stringified-json>"}}`
+   - **OpenAI tool_calls array:** `{"tool_calls": [{"function": {"name": "...", "arguments": "<stringified-json>"}}]}`
+   - **Anthropic tool_use:** `{"type": "tool_use", "name": "...", "input": {...}}`
+   - **Improvised "qwen with no schemas":** `{"call_tool": {"name": "...", "arguments": "<stringified-json>"}}` â€” what qwen3.6 emitted in our log. Likely an artifact of running with no tools registered (no schemas in the chat template â†’ model invents a wrapper). Once the registration task lands, this exact shape may go away, but parsing it is cheap insurance.
+
+**What to change:**
+
+1. **Pre-strip pass before any JSON parser runs.** In `JsonToolCallParser` (or a wrapper around it), before attempting `serde_json::from_str`:
+   - Remove `<think>...</think>` blocks (greedy or non-greedy is fine â€” Qwen reasoning blocks always close).
+   - Remove markdown code fences. Treat ```json, ```, and bare-language fences identically â€” strip the opening fence and the matching closing fence; keep the inner content.
+   - Trim outer whitespace and prose. Heuristic: find the first `{` and the last `}` of the trimmed inner content; attempt to parse that span as JSON. If it fails, also try the entire trimmed text. Don't over-engineer; two attempts is enough.
+
+2. **Add wrapper recognition to the JSON parser.** After parsing succeeds, normalize across the four-or-five wrapper shapes above into the parser's internal `ToolCall { name, arguments }` representation. `arguments` should accept either a JSON object or a string-encoded JSON object (some shapes stringify, some don't). This is straightforward serde matching â€” try each shape until one fits, then map.
+
+3. **Detection update for plain Qwen3.** In `detect_from_model_name`, recognize plain `qwen3` (and `qwen3.x`) â€” currently it only fires `Qwen3Coder` for `qwen3 + coder`. Add a `Qwen3` (Hermes) variant or, more simply, expand `Default`'s parsers to be lenient enough that detection doesn't matter for this case. Either is fine; the lenient-Default path is the lower-risk change.
+
+4. **Don't break Qwen3-Coder.** That strategy's XML format is more elaborate (per the official docs and `llama.cpp` issue [#15012](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/issues/15012)) and stays as-is. Adding leniency to the JSON path doesn't touch it.
+
+**Tests required:**
+
+- Unit tests with each of the five wrapper shapes above as input strings, with and without ```json fences, with and without leading `<think>...</think>` blocks, with and without surrounding prose. Each must parse to the same `ToolCall { name, arguments }`.
+- The literal qwen3.6-27B output captured from our log on 2026-04-25 must parse: ` ```json\n{"call_tool":{"arguments":"{\"file_path\": \"...\"}","name":"read_file"}}\n``` `.
+- Negative cases: a plain narrative response with no JSON at all must still return "no tool calls" (not crash, not falsely match). A JSON object that isn't a tool-call shape (e.g. `{"status": "passed"}`) must NOT be matched as a tool call.
+
+**Why:** The `<think>â€¦</think>` reasoning block + ```json fenced response is the dominant pattern for current Qwen3-class models. A parser that doesn't strip those is a parser that doesn't work for the most common modern open-weight tool user. We see this in our own log; vLLM upstream sees it too ([vllm-project/vllm#31871](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/31871) â€” Hermes streaming returning raw `<tool_call>` text instead of parsed calls).
+
+**Pairs with:** Validator-side tool registration (separate task). Even a perfect parser won't help if the model has no tool schemas to call against â€” qwen's improvised `{"call_tool": ...}` format in our log is exactly the artifact of an empty tool registry. The two fixes are complementary; do both, in either order, but neither alone solves the end-to-end problem.
+
+**Sources:**
+- [Qwen3.6-27B official blog](https://qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.6-27b)
+- [Qwen Function Calling docs](https://qwen.readthedocs.io/en/latest/framework/function_call.html)
+- [vLLM Tool Calling docs](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/features/tool_calling/)
+- [Qwen3.6-27B HuggingFace card](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B)
+- [llama.cpp Qwen3-Coder parser feature request](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/issues/15012)
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Lenient tool-call JSON parsing in llama-agent (strip fences, drop think tags, accept multiple wrappers)

**Observed:** Qwen3.6-27B (via `unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF`) emitted a tool-call attempt that the validator runner saw as raw text:

```
<think>
The user wants me to validate ... I need to read the file ...
</think>

```json
{"call_tool":{"arguments":"{\"file_path\": \"...sample_avp_test.rs\"}","name":"read_file"}}
```
```

llama-agent's `Default` strategy (chosen because the model name `Qwen3.6-27B` does not match the `qwen3 + coder` predicate in `detect_from_model_name`) ran JsonToolCallParser, XmlToolCallParser, FunctionCallParser in sequence and matched none of them â€” so the agent reported "No tool calls detected" and the text bubbled all the way up to `avp_common::validator::executor` as the final assistant response.

**Where it lives:** `llama-agent/src/chat_template.rs` â€” `ToolParsingStrategy` enum, `detect_from_model_name`, the parsers registered in `ChatTemplateEngine::new()` (`json`, `xml`, `function_call`).

**Two compounding parser-coverage gaps:**

1. **JSON parser is brittle about wrapping.** Models in practice emit JSON inside markdown code fences (```json â€¦ ```), inside `<think>â€¦</think>` reasoning blocks (Qwen3 series), interleaved with prose, or with leading/trailing whitespace and natural-language explanation. A "JSON parser" worth the name should be forgiving in what it accepts. Today, anything other than a bare top-level JSON object is missed.

2. **Wrapper-shape coverage is incomplete.** Even after stripping fences/think tags, the JSON object inside can take several shapes in the wild:
   - **Hermes / canonical Qwen3:** `{"name": "...", "arguments": {...}}` â€” the format Qwen3.6 emits when the chat template injects tool schemas. Wrapped in `<tool_call>...</tool_call>` per the tokenizer config. ([qwen.readthedocs.io function_call docs](https://qwen.readthedocs.io/en/latest/framework/function_call.html), [vLLM tool_call_parser hermes](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/features/tool_calling/))
   - **OpenAI legacy function_call:** `{"function_call": {"name": "...", "arguments": "<stringified-json>"}}`
   - **OpenAI tool_calls array:** `{"tool_calls": [{"function": {"name": "...", "arguments": "<stringified-json>"}}]}`
   - **Anthropic tool_use:** `{"type": "tool_use", "name": "...", "input": {...}}`
   - **Improvised "qwen with no schemas":** `{"call_tool": {"name": "...", "arguments": "<stringified-json>"}}` â€” what qwen3.6 emitted in our log. Likely an artifact of running with no tools registered (no schemas in the chat template â†’ model invents a wrapper). Once the registration task lands, this exact shape may go away, but parsing it is cheap insurance.

**What to change:**

1. **Pre-strip pass before any JSON parser runs.** In `JsonToolCallParser` (or a wrapper around it), before attempting `serde_json::from_str`:
   - Remove `<think>...</think>` blocks (greedy or non-greedy is fine â€” Qwen reasoning blocks always close).
   - Remove markdown code fences. Treat ```json, ```, and bare-language fences identically â€” strip the opening fence and the matching closing fence; keep the inner content.
   - Trim outer whitespace and prose. Heuristic: find the first `{` and the last `}` of the trimmed inner content; attempt to parse that span as JSON. If it fails, also try the entire trimmed text. Don't over-engineer; two attempts is enough.

2. **Add wrapper recognition to the JSON parser.** After parsing succeeds, normalize across the four-or-five wrapper shapes above into the parser's internal `ToolCall { name, arguments }` representation. `arguments` should accept either a JSON object or a string-encoded JSON object (some shapes stringify, some don't). This is straightforward serde matching â€” try each shape until one fits, then map.

3. **Detection update for plain Qwen3.** In `detect_from_model_name`, recognize plain `qwen3` (and `qwen3.x`) â€” currently it only fires `Qwen3Coder` for `qwen3 + coder`. Add a `Qwen3` (Hermes) variant or, more simply, expand `Default`'s parsers to be lenient enough that detection doesn't matter for this case. Either is fine; the lenient-Default path is the lower-risk change.

4. **Don't break Qwen3-Coder.** That strategy's XML format is more elaborate (per the official docs and `llama.cpp` issue [#15012](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/issues/15012)) and stays as-is. Adding leniency to the JSON path doesn't touch it.

**Tests required:**

- Unit tests with each of the five wrapper shapes above as input strings, with and without ```json fences, with and without leading `<think>...</think>` blocks, with and without surrounding prose. Each must parse to the same `ToolCall { name, arguments }`.
- The literal qwen3.6-27B output captured from our log on 2026-04-25 must parse: ` ```json\n{"call_tool":{"arguments":"{\"file_path\": \"...\"}","name":"read_file"}}\n``` `.
- Negative cases: a plain narrative response with no JSON at all must still return "no tool calls" (not crash, not falsely match). A JSON object that isn't a tool-call shape (e.g. `{"status": "passed"}`) must NOT be matched as a tool call.

**Why:** The `<think>â€¦</think>` reasoning block + ```json fenced response is the dominant pattern for current Qwen3-class models. A parser that doesn't strip those is a parser that doesn't work for the most common modern open-weight tool user. We see this in our own log; vLLM upstream sees it too ([vllm-project/vllm#31871](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/31871) â€” Hermes streaming returning raw `<tool_call>` text instead of parsed calls).

**Pairs with:** Validator-side tool registration (separate task). Even a perfect parser won't help if the model has no tool schemas to call against â€” qwen's improvised `{"call_tool": ...}` format in our log is exactly the artifact of an empty tool registry. The two fixes are complementary; do both, in either order, but neither alone solves the end-to-end problem.

**Sources:**
- [Qwen3.6-27B official blog](https://qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.6-27b)
- [Qwen Function Calling docs](https://qwen.readthedocs.io/en/latest/framework/function_call.html)
- [vLLM Tool Calling docs](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/features/tool_calling/)
- [Qwen3.6-27B HuggingFace card](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3.6-27B)

 ---
-**Observed:** Qwen3.6-27B (via `unsloth/Qwen3.6-27B-GGUF`) emitted a tool-call attempt that the validator runner saw as raw text:
+**Belongs entirely in llama-agent.** Per the agent-client-protocol contract, `avp-common` only hands the agent an MCP tool list and expects the right thing to come back through `prompt()`. How the agent renders that tool list into the system message and how it parses tool calls out of model output is a llama-agent concern, model-keyed by `ToolParsingStrategy`.
+
+## The gap
+
+Today the strategy enum in `llama-agent/src/chat_template.rs` is `Default | Qwen3Coder | OpenAI | Claude`, and `detect_from_model_name` only fires `Qwen3Coder` when the model name contains both `qwen3` AND `coder`. Plain `qwen3` / `qwen3.6` falls through to `Default` â€” a one-size-fits-all strategy that:
+
+- On the **input side** (`format_tools_for_template`, around line 568), renders tools in a generic format that the Qwen3-Instruct chat template wasn't trained on for arbitrary tools-without-schemas.
+- On the **output side**, runs `JsonToolCallParser`, `XmlToolCallParser`, `FunctionCallParser` in sequence â€” none of which targets `<tool_call>...</tool_call>` Hermes wrappers.
+
+Result: even when avp wires tools correctly via MCP, qwen3.6 either improvises a JSON shape (because its system message didn't show it canonical Hermes schemas) or emits canonical `<tool_call>` wrappers that the parser ignores. Both halves need work and they pair up.
+
+## Observed (2026-04-25 qwen test run)

 ```
 <think>
@@ -17,47 +28,87 @@
 ```
 ```

-llama-agent's `Default` strategy (chosen because the model name `Qwen3.6-27B` does not match the `qwen3 + coder` predicate in `detect_from_model_name`) ran JsonToolCallParser, XmlToolCallParser, FunctionCallParser in sequence and matched none of them â€” so the agent reported "No tool calls detected" and the text bubbled all the way up to `avp_common::validator::executor` as the final assistant response.
+The `Default` strategy missed this; the agent reported "0 tool calls executed."

-**Where it lives:** `llama-agent/src/chat_template.rs` â€” `ToolParsingStrategy` enum, `detect_from_model_name`, the parsers registered in `ChatTemplateEngine::new()` (`json`, `xml`, `function_call`).
+## What to change

-**Two compounding parser-coverage gaps:**
+### 1. New strategy: `Qwen3` (Hermes-style)

-1. **JSON parser is brittle about wrapping.** Models in practice emit JSON inside markdown code fences (```json â€¦ ```), inside `<think>â€¦</think>` reasoning blocks (Qwen3 series), interleaved with prose, or with leading/trailing whitespace and natural-language explanation. A "JSON parser" worth the name should be forgiving in what it accepts. Today, anything other than a bare top-level JSON object is missed.
+Add a `Qwen3` variant to `ToolParsingStrategy`. Distinct from `Qwen3Coder` (XML), `OpenAI` (function calling), `Claude` (tool_use). Hermes is the canonical format for plain Qwen3 / Qwen3.6 / Qwen3-Instruct.

-2. **Wrapper-shape coverage is incomplete.** Even after stripping fences/think tags, the JSON object inside can take several shapes in the wild:
-   - **Hermes / canonical Qwen3:** `{"name": "...", "arguments": {...}}` â€” the format Qwen3.6 emits when the chat template injects tool schemas. Wrapped in `<tool_call>...</tool_call>` per the tokenizer config. ([qwen.readthedocs.io function_call docs](https://qwen.readthedocs.io/en/latest/framework/function_call.html), [vLLM tool_call_parser hermes](https://docs.vllm.ai/en/latest/features/tool_calling/))
-   - **OpenAI legacy function_call:** `{"function_call": {"name": "...", "arguments": "<stringified-json>"}}`
-   - **OpenAI tool_calls array:** `{"tool_calls": [{"function": {"name": "...", "arguments": "<stringified-json>"}}]}`
-   - **Anthropic tool_use:** `{"type": "tool_use", "name": "...", "input": {...}}`
-   - **Improvised "qwen with no schemas":** `{"call_tool": {"name": "...", "arguments": "<stringified-json>"}}` â€” what qwen3.6 emitted in our log. Likely an artifact of running with no tools registered (no schemas in the chat template â†’ model invents a wrapper). Once the registration task lands, this exact shape may go away, but parsing it is cheap insurance.
+### 2. Detection

-**What to change:**
+Update `detect_from_model_name`:
+- `qwen3` AND `coder` â†’ `Qwen3Coder` (existing).
+- `qwen3` (anywhere â€” including `qwen3.6`, `qwen3-instruct`, `qwen3-30b`, etc.) without `coder` â†’ `Qwen3`.
+- All others unchanged.

-1. **Pre-strip pass before any JSON parser runs.** In `JsonToolCallParser` (or a wrapper around it), before attempting `serde_json::from_str`:
-   - Remove `<think>...</think>` blocks (greedy or non-greedy is fine â€” Qwen reasoning blocks always close).
-   - Remove markdown code fences. Treat ```json, ```, and bare-language fences identically â€” strip the opening fence and the matching closing fence; keep the inner content.
-   - Trim outer whitespace and prose. Heuristic: find the first `{` and the last `}` of the trimmed inner content; attempt to parse that span as JSON. If it fails, also try the entire trimmed text. Don't over-engineer; two attempts is enough.
+### 3. Input rendering (system-message side)

-2. **Add wrapper recognition to the JSON parser.** After parsing succeeds, normalize across the four-or-five wrapper shapes above into the parser's internal `ToolCall { name, arguments }` representation. `arguments` should accept either a JSON object or a string-encoded JSON object (some shapes stringify, some don't). This is straightforward serde matching â€” try each shape until one fits, then map.
+When the strategy is `Qwen3`, `format_tools_for_template` must produce Hermes-formatted tool schemas â€” the format the Qwen3 tokenizer chat template was trained on. Pattern (from `qwen.readthedocs.io` and the Qwen3 tokenizer config):

-3. **Detection update for plain Qwen3.** In `detect_from_model_name`, recognize plain `qwen3` (and `qwen3.x`) â€” currently it only fires `Qwen3Coder` for `qwen3 + coder`. Add a `Qwen3` (Hermes) variant or, more simply, expand `Default`'s parsers to be lenient enough that detection doesn't matter for this case. Either is fine; the lenient-Default path is the lower-risk change.
+```
+# Tools

-4. **Don't break Qwen3-Coder.** That strategy's XML format is more elaborate (per the official docs and `llama.cpp` issue [#15012](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/issues/15012)) and stays as-is. Adding leniency to the JSON path doesn't touch it.
+You may call one or more functions to assist with the user query.

-**Tests required:**
+You are provided with function signatures within <tools></tools> XML tags:
+<tools>
+{"type":"function","function":{"name":"read_file","description":"...","parameters":{...}}}
+{"type":"function","function":{"name":"glob","description":"...","parameters":{...}}}
+</tools>

-- Unit tests with each of the five wrapper shapes above as input strings, with and without ```json fences, with and without leading `<think>...</think>` blocks, with and without surrounding prose. Each must parse to the same `ToolCall { name, arguments }`.
-- The literal qwen3.6-27B output captured from our log on 2026-04-25 must parse: ` ```json\n{"call_tool":{"arguments":"{\"file_path\": \"...\"}","name":"read_file"}}\n``` `.
-- Negative cases: a plain narrative response with no JSON at all must still return "no tool calls" (not crash, not falsely match). A JSON object that isn't a tool-call shape (e.g. `{"status": "passed"}`) must NOT be matched as a tool call.
+For each function call, return a json object with function name and arguments within <tool_call></tool_call> XML tags:
+<tool_call>
+{"name": "<function-name>", "arguments": <args-json-object>}
+</tool_call>
+```
+
+Confirm the exact wording against `Qwen/Qwen3-8B`'s `tokenizer_config.json` chat template before shipping â€” small wording differences matter for in-distribution behavior.
+
+### 4. Output parsing (model-output side)
+
+When the strategy is `Qwen3`, the parser pipeline targets the Hermes shape and is robust to common model-output noise:
+
+- **Strip reasoning blocks first.** Remove any `<think>...</think>` regions before further parsing. Qwen3 reasoning models always close their think tags. Don't try to parse JSON inside think blocks.
+- **Find `<tool_call>...</tool_call>` regions.** Inside, parse the JSON object â€” `{"name": "...", "arguments": {...}}` (object, not stringified). Return one `ToolCall` per region.
+- **Markdown-fence tolerance as a safety net.** Some models / quantizations / fine-tunes emit the JSON inside ```json ... ``` fences instead of (or alongside) `<tool_call>` tags. Strip ```json / ``` / bare-language fences before the JSON parse attempt. Don't rely on this â€” once registration is correct (avp side), `<tool_call>` should be the dominant shape.
+- **Fallback wrapper shapes (cheap insurance).** After fence-stripping, if the JSON object looks like a tool call but uses a different wrapper, recognize it: `{"function_call": {...}}` (OpenAI legacy), `{"tool_calls": [...]}` (OpenAI array), `{"type":"tool_use","name":"...","input":{...}}` (Anthropic), `{"call_tool": {"name":"...","arguments":"<stringified>"}}` (the improvised qwen-without-schemas shape we saw in our log). `arguments` accepts either an object or a stringified-JSON string.
+- **Negative cases must still return "no tool calls."** A plain narrative response with no JSON, or a JSON object that isn't tool-call-shaped (e.g. `{"status": "passed"}`), MUST NOT be falsely matched.
+
+### 5. Don't break Qwen3-Coder
+
+The existing `Qwen3Coder` strategy stays as-is. Its XML format is more elaborate (per the Qwen3-Coder docs and `llama.cpp` issue [#15012](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/issues/15012)). Detection precedence is `Qwen3Coder` before `Qwen3` (the more specific match wins).
+
+## Tests required
+
+- Unit tests in `chat_template.rs`:
+  - `detect_from_model_name("Qwen3.6-27B")` â†’ `Qwen3`.
+  - `detect_from_model_name("Qwen/Qwen3-8B-Instruct")` â†’ `Qwen3`.
+  - `detect_from_model_name("unsloth/Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B-Instruct-GGUF")` â†’ `Qwen3Coder` (regression â€” the more specific match wins).
+- Input-rendering unit tests: with `Qwen3` strategy and a small `Vec<ToolDefinition>`, confirm `format_tools_for_template` produces output containing `<tools>`, the function JSON, and `<tool_call>` example markers.
+- Output-parsing unit tests for each shape:
+  - Canonical: `<tool_call>{"name":"read_file","arguments":{"file_path":"a.rs"}}</tool_call>` â†’ 1 ToolCall.
+  - With reasoning prefix: `<think>...</think>\n<tool_call>...</tool_call>` â†’ 1 ToolCall (reasoning stripped).
+  - Markdown-fenced: ` ```json\n{"name":"read_file","arguments":{"file_path":"a.rs"}}\n``` ` â†’ 1 ToolCall.
+  - Improvised wrapper from our log: ` ```json\n{"call_tool":{"arguments":"{\"file_path\": \"a.rs\"}","name":"read_file"}}\n``` ` â†’ 1 ToolCall.
+  - Multiple tool calls in one response: two `<tool_call>` regions â†’ 2 ToolCalls.
+  - Negative: `{"status":"passed","message":"ok"}` â†’ 0 ToolCalls.
+  - Negative: plain English narrative â†’ 0 ToolCalls.
+
+## Acceptance
+
+- A real qwen3.6-27B run with tools registered (driven by avp via MCP, but verified at the llama-agent layer) emits `<tool_call>` shapes that the new parser captures, and the agent loop dispatches the tool through MCP and feeds the result back. Validated by inspecting llama-agent traces / the recordings produced by `01KQ369KBDK6Y5DRN53WB7FDXQ`.
+- Existing strategies (`Default`, `Qwen3Coder`, `OpenAI`, `Claude`) regress in zero tests.
+- `cargo test -p llama-agent` and `cargo clippy -p llama-agent --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.

-**Why:** The `<think>â€¦</think>` reasoning block + ```json fenced response is the dominant pattern for current Qwen3-class models. A parser that doesn't strip those is a parser that doesn't work for the most common modern open-weight tool user. We see this in our own log; vLLM upstream sees it too ([vllm-project/vllm#31871](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/31871) â€” Hermes streaming returning raw `<tool_call>` text instead of parsed calls).
+## Pairs with (avp side)

-**Pairs with:** Validator-side tool registration (separate task). Even a perfect parser won't help if the model has no tool schemas to call against â€” qwen's improvised `{"call_tool": ...}` format in our log is exactly the artifact of an empty tool registry. The two fixes are complementary; do both, in either order, but neither alone solves the end-to-end problem.
+`01KQ35MHFJQPMEKQ08PZKBKFY0` (validator tools wiring + SAH_HTTP_PORT fallback). avp's only job is putting tools in front of the agent; this task makes plain Qwen3 actually use them.

-**Sources:**
-- [Qwen3.6-27B official blog](https://qwen.ai/blog?id=qwen3.6-27b)
